tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Perform click on a web page

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: button
arguments.desc: Button to click, defaults to left

arguments.name: doubleClick
arguments.desc: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

arguments.name: modifiers
arguments.desc: Modifier keys to press

tools.name: browser_close
tools.description: Close the page

tools.name: browser_console_messages
tools.description: Returns all console messages

arguments.name: level
arguments.desc: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

arguments.name: all
arguments.desc: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

arguments.name: endTarget
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: startTarget
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: endElement
arguments.desc: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

arguments.name: startElement
arguments.desc: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

arguments.name: function
arguments.desc: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_file_upload
tools.description: Upload one or multiple files

arguments.name: paths
arguments.desc: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

tools.name: browser_fill_form
tools.description: Fill multiple form fields

arguments.name: fields
arguments.desc: Fields to fill in

tools.name: browser_handle_dialog
tools.description: Handle a dialog

arguments.name: accept
arguments.desc: Whether to accept the dialog.

arguments.name: promptText
arguments.desc: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover over element on page

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: The URL to navigate to

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: Go back to the previous page in the history

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: Returns all network requests since loading the page

arguments.name: requestBody
arguments.desc: Whether to include request body. Defaults to false.

arguments.name: requestHeaders
arguments.desc: Whether to include request headers. Defaults to false.

arguments.name: static
arguments.desc: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.desc: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

tools.name: browser_press_key
tools.description: Press a key on the keyboard

arguments.name: key
arguments.desc: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

tools.name: browser_resize
tools.description: Resize the browser window

arguments.name: height
arguments.desc: Height of the browser window

arguments.name: width
arguments.desc: Width of the browser window

tools.name: browser_run_code
tools.description: Run Playwright code snippet

arguments.name: code
arguments.desc: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a dropdown

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: values
arguments.desc: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

arguments.name: depth
arguments.desc: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_tabs
tools.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

arguments.name: action
arguments.desc: Operation to perform

arguments.name: index
arguments.desc: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

arguments.name: type
arguments.desc: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

arguments.name: filename
arguments.desc: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

arguments.name: fullPage
arguments.desc: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into editable element

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

arguments.name: text
arguments.desc: Text to type into the element

arguments.name: element
arguments.desc: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

arguments.name: slowly
arguments.desc: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

arguments.name: submit
arguments.desc: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

arguments.name: text
arguments.desc: The text to wait for

arguments.name: textGone
arguments.desc: The text to wait for to disappear

arguments.name: time
arguments.desc: The time to wait in seconds

server.name: fetch
server.description: Fetches a URL from the internet and extracts its contents as markdown.
server.title: Fetch (Reference)

tools.name: fetch
tools.description:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown.

Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: URL to fetch

arguments.name: max_length
arguments.desc: Maximum number of characters to return.

arguments.name: raw
arguments.desc: Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification.

arguments.name: start_index
arguments.desc: On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required.

server.name: youtube_transcript
server.description: Retrieves transcripts for given YouTube video URLs.
server.title: YouTube Transcripts

tools.name: get_available_languages
tools.description: Retrieves the available languages for the video.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: The URL of the YouTube video

tools.name: get_timed_transcript
tools.description: Retrieves the transcript of a YouTube video with timestamps.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: The URL of the YouTube video

arguments.name: lang
arguments.desc: The preferred language for the transcript

arguments.name: next_cursor
arguments.desc: Cursor to retrieve the next page of the transcript

tools.name: get_transcript
tools.description: Retrieves the transcript of a YouTube video.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: The URL of the YouTube video

arguments.name: lang
arguments.desc: The preferred language for the transcript

arguments.name: next_cursor
arguments.desc: Cursor to retrieve the next page of the transcript

tools.name: get_video_info
tools.description: Retrieves the video information.

arguments.name: url
arguments.desc: The URL of the YouTube video

server.name: filesystem
server.description: Local filesystem access with configurable allowed paths.
server.title: Filesystem (Reference)

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation. If the directory already exists, this operation will succeed silently. Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.description: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure. Each entry includes 'name', 'type' (file/directory), and 'children' for directories. Files have no children array, while directories always have a children array (which may be empty). The output is formatted with 2-space indentation for readability. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Make line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces exact line sequences with new content. Returns a git-style diff showing the changes made. Only works within allowed directories.

arguments.name: dryRun
arguments.desc: Preview changes using git-style diff format

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory. Returns comprehensive information including size, creation time, last modified time, permissions, and type. This tool is perfect for understanding file characteristics without reading the actual content. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: list_allowed_directories
tools.description: Returns the list of directories that this server is allowed to access. Use this to understand which directories are available before trying to access files.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path. Results clearly distinguish between files and directories with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. This tool is essential for understanding directory structure and finding specific files within a directory. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename files and directories. Can move files between directories and rename them in a single operation. If the destination exists, the operation will fail. Works across different directories and can be used for simple renaming within the same directory. Both source and destination must be within allowed directories.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously. This is more efficient than reading files one by one when you need to analyze or compare multiple files. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Failed reads for individual files won't stop the entire operation. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Recursively search for files and directories matching a pattern. Searches through all subdirectories from the starting path. The search is case-insensitive and matches partial names. Returns full paths to all matching items. Great for finding files when you don't know their exact location. Only searches within allowed directories.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content. Use with caution as it will overwrite existing files without warning. Handles text content with proper encoding. Only works within allowed directories.

config.description: The MCP server is allowed to access these paths
config.name: filesystem

server.name: obsidian
server.description: MCP server that interacts with Obsidian via the Obsidian rest API community plugin.
server.title: Obsidian

tools.name: obsidian_append_content
tools.description: Append content to a new or existing file in the vault.

arguments.name: content
arguments.desc: Content to append to the file

arguments.name: filepath
arguments.desc: Path to the file (relative to vault root)

tools.name: obsidian_batch_get_file_contents
tools.description: Return the contents of multiple files in your vault, concatenated with headers.

arguments.name: filepaths
arguments.desc: List of file paths to read

tools.name: obsidian_complex_search
tools.description:
Complex search for documents using a JsonLogic query. 
           Supports standard JsonLogic operators plus 'glob' and 'regexp' for pattern matching. Results must be non-falsy.

           Use this tool when you want to do a complex search, e.g. for all documents with certain tags etc.

arguments.name: query
arguments.desc: JsonLogic query object. Example: {"glob": ["*.md", {"var": "path"}]} matches all markdown files

tools.name: obsidian_delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file or directory from the vault.

arguments.name: confirm
arguments.desc: Confirmation to delete the file (must be true)

arguments.name: filepath
arguments.desc: Path to the file or directory to delete (relative to vault root)

tools.name: obsidian_get_file_contents
tools.description: Return the content of a single file in your vault.

arguments.name: filepath
arguments.desc: Path to the relevant file (relative to your vault root).

tools.name: obsidian_get_periodic_note
tools.description: Get current periodic note for the specified period.

arguments.name: period
arguments.desc: The period type (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)

tools.name: obsidian_get_recent_changes
tools.description: Get recently modified files in the vault.

arguments.name: days
arguments.desc: Only include files modified within this many days (default: 90)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Maximum number of files to return (default: 10)

tools.name: obsidian_get_recent_periodic_notes
tools.description: Get most recent periodic notes for the specified period type.

arguments.name: period
arguments.desc: The period type (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)

arguments.name: include_content
arguments.desc: Whether to include note content (default: false)

arguments.name: limit
arguments.desc: Maximum number of notes to return (default: 5)

tools.name: obsidian_list_files_in_dir
tools.description: Lists all files and directories that exist in a specific Obsidian directory.

arguments.name: dirpath
arguments.desc: Path to list files from (relative to your vault root). Note that empty directories will not be returned.

tools.name: obsidian_list_files_in_vault
tools.description: Lists all files and directories in the root directory of your Obsidian vault.

tools.name: obsidian_patch_content
tools.description: Insert content into an existing note relative to a heading, block reference, or frontmatter field.

arguments.name: content
arguments.desc: Content to insert

arguments.name: filepath
arguments.desc: Path to the file (relative to vault root)

arguments.name: operation
arguments.desc: Operation to perform (append, prepend, or replace)

arguments.name: target
arguments.desc: Target identifier (heading path, block reference, or frontmatter field)

arguments.name: target_type
arguments.desc: Type of target to patch

tools.name: obsidian_simple_search
tools.description:
Simple search for documents matching a specified text query across all files in the vault. 
            Use this tool when you want to do a simple text search

arguments.name: query
arguments.desc: Text to a simple search for in the vault.

arguments.name: context_length
arguments.desc: How much context to return around the matching string (default: 100)
